You know, Spock was removed from captaincy cause he was emotional compromised, but this Kirk is ABSOLUTELY not emotionally compromised.

Spock comes to because his dad said he loved his wife, and sets himself as the new first officer. Status quo achieved!

Loud flicky pew pew action scene, oh boy. Scotty beams them to what he thinks is an unoccupied part of the ship. It’s not. Fight time. Luckily all these enemies are very bad. They’re attacking JUST as the ship starts to bore a hole in Earth like it did for Vulcan, almost blowing up the Golden Gate Bridge. Been a while since we saw it blow up! Also now that Spock and Kirk have fought together in a SINGLE fight, Spock is on a first name basis with Kirk and calls him Jim. This movie has some problems with pacing.

Nero: “I WILL DEPRIVE YOU OF YOUR LIFE, JUST LIKE I DID YOUR FATHER”

How the shit did you know that you killed his dad, you never interracted with him. Okay, man. It’s not like I’ve never made writing plothole mistakes before but whoof. This villain isn’t TERRIBLE, but I don’t like him that much. This is what everyone thinks Shinzon from Nemesis is.

Spock is going to sacrifice himself and blow up the red matter, but the Enterprise comes in like Big Damn Heroes to save the day. He crashes the ship with the red matter into Nero’s ship, creating a black hole, just as Kirk saves Pike and Spock is warped away. Was Spock trying to kill Kirk again while doing this? Yes. Yes, he was straight up going to kill him.

Wow this movie has a lot of flashing effects.

THey eject the warp core, and by that I mean like 8 of them, I dunno why they have 8 of them, and let the blast carry them away from the singularity. What if we had more flashing and more shaking effects? Lovely. And then they surfed the wave while listening to rock music. Wait, wrong movie.

We get a goodbye to Old!Spock as he meets Young!Spock. He explains he lied about it, because it’d make Kirk and Spock be friends. Fair enough.

All the cadets are still there, despite the fact that ALL of the ships they were all put in were blown the fuck up earlier in the movie, sure, okay, that’s fine. They make Kirk captain, give him a medal, and all cheer him on. Also Pike is in a wheelchair now! All is right in the fucking world.

The movie ends with them blasting off. Punch it chewie, etc. And then we get Old!Spock doing the voice over. These are the missions of etc etc. That’s pretty emotional. And we get a reprise of the original theme! Cool.

Obviously this movie is like, not great. It’s kinda bad, in many ways. But it’s not unenjoyable? My friend Elle in my group watch said it best. “This movie is a 40 minute carnival ride that lasts for two hours”. It is shlocky action that is poorly directed, because JJ Abrahms is not a good director. Also we all make fun of the lens flares and weird scaling but the REAL problem is that it was loud, too fast, and the fucking flashing was nuts. This movie is so bright, and so loud. It’s like getting flashbanged by a mediocre book.

At least the soundtrack is pretty good.

Star Trek: Into Darkness - 1/10

Let’s get this over with.

We start with a scene with Kirk being chased by some indigenous aliens. This feels racist somehow. Spock is trying to put a Super Icecube in asuper volcano that’s about to erupt and kill their planet. Spock is also complaining about the Prime Directive, which is weird cause it didn’t exist at this point but I’m honestly just surprised JJ Abrahms knows what that is. Uhura is there to kiss Spock’s helmet. Why is she on this mission? She’s the comms officer.

These aliens live on the Silent Hill F world.

Kirk violates the prime directive to save Spock’s life and the aliens literally begin to form a cult around the Enterprise. Lovely.

We get like a 2 minute weird scene of people mourning someone then the sexiest man ever for some reason, perriwinkle bandersnatch, shows up and is like “I CAN SAY HER”

Remember as you watch this movie, JJ’s actual advice: speak quickly enough so everyone thinks what you’re saying is smart. Kirk’s got that to a T.

Kirk is fired for doing this, which is a thing admirals are able to do I guess. I thought there was a method for this, it’s called like, a Coort Marshull? Something like that. Also yeah no shit Kirk is bad at this, he was an Ensign for literally one day before you made him captain for some fucking reason.

Why are the Starfleet uniforms just imperial outfits? Why are they so fashy?

There’s a terrorist bombing, some dude blows some place up. After it explodes it zooms into an apartment, and then it zooms in on the picture of some kid. How melodramatic, we don’t even know who the fuck this is

Pike was given the Enterprise back. What? Was he demoted from Admiral?!?! He’s also going to make Kirk his XO, and Spock is reassigned. Anyways, they are assigned to find the group behind the bombing, when suddenly a ship shoots up their conference room.

Holee shiiiiitttt, I thought the LAST movie had bad flashing, I think they made it worse

For some reason as Pike dies, Spock mind melds with him. That seems dangerous AND like a violation.

Bandersnatch is beamed from one planet to another which is apparently something you can do now. God, the dumb nit picky shit is even WORSE in this. Honestly everything is. The writing is worse, too.

Watching Spock and Kirk bicker is not enjoyable. Kirk wants to assassinate Bandersnatch, who’s apparently a commander named Harrison, who is hiding on Kronos with a drone strike. Spock thinks its sus. Scotty thinks its sus. Kirk has a murder boner, so Scotty straight up resigns. Go be in a better movie, Simon Pegg.

We find out WHY Spock mind melded with Pike. He wanted to see what it felt to die. What the FUCK, dude??? Anyways they’re IMMEDIATELY found by some Klingons, and Uhura is like “They’re gonna torture and kill us ):” yeah girlie. What did you THINK was gonna happen if you sneak into their airspace with a missile designed to kill?

Why do the klingons wear predator masks? Is it so they didn’t have to show them without cranial ridges, cause of all THAT lore?

No, they just redesigned the Klingons anyway. Why do I even bother.

Holy shit this movie is so fucking bright. There’s a big fight scene cause this terrorist started firing, and then after the Klingons leave, he surrenders. Then Kirk beats the shit out of him. Very captainy of him.

holy fisheye lens, batman.

Holy shit, first trans-dimensional beaming that lets you beam across the galaxy, now they have cellphones that can communicate across the galaxy too?? Kirk calls Scotty to get his help after Harrison whispers some bullshit in his ear. I’m checked out.

We’re introduced to a woman who is connected to this somehow and basically snuck onto the Enterprise. She’s Carol Marcus, who is in fact from the other movies. She also namedrops nurse Chapel. Clap, nerds! Her dad is Robocop, which is weirdly enough the second time I’ve seen him this week, cause he was in the final few episodes of Enterprise, which I also watched around this time.

Oh wow, Bandersnatch is actually Khan! CLAP, NERDS! There’s SO MUCH shit about this. Like, first of all, the scene is bad. Every decision is wrong. But also the shit about him being Khan was weirdly hidden around the time even though we all knew, it was obvious. Also Khan was not a pasty british white guy but whatever. Khan tries to get their sympathy by going “I am such a sad boy”. Kirk probably failed history class, but Spock SHOULD have said “You enslaved and killed millions. No.”

He says Marcus’ dad, an admiral, is trying to take over the Federation and start a war with the Klingons and used him to make super weapons. Alright. Marcus shows up in a random ship, catches up to to THE FLAGSHIP OF THE FEDERATION, and EASILY blasts them out of hyperspace. Okay, sure. They try to use Carol as a gambit and she’s just beamed off the ship anyway. Marcus, again played by Robocop (they made this guy a fascist villain twice in a row?) is going to blow up the Enterprise, but Scotty saved them by sabotaging the ship (which btw is called the USS Vengeance. Jesus christ.)

Anyways they team up with Khan. Glad he’s a good guy :) Why is this movie still going, it feels so long. At least Scotty is back in the movie, he’s still the best part.

“Sorry about this,” Scotty says before killing a guy. Do you think that makes it better?

Leonard Nimoy’s last Trek scene is him going “Oh yeah, Khan? He’s bad news, don’t trust that fucker”. Rest in peace, man.

This movie is seriously filled with so many “He said the thing, clap, nerds” things in it that it blows my mind. In any other movie that’s a remake/retelling of a previous classic beloved movie, I’d assume it’s just cause the people making it love that movie so much. I genuinely do not believe that JJ Abrahms has ever seen an episode of Star Trek, he was just given a list of data about it and read some message boards.

Woah, Khan betrayed them, what a FUCKING SHOCK. And then he betrays them AGAIN after promising to let them survive if they give him what he wants. And the entire time, Bandersnatch is SO BAD at- wait, they lowered shields to let him teleport what he wants, his crew, and then when he fires on Enterprise, they go “SHIELDS ARE DOWN TO 20%”. What.

btw this is over Luna. I mean, come on, it’s only THE MOON OF EARTH. Anyways they outsmart Khan and blow up his ship, but they’re damaged so they begin to fall to Earth. And they fall from Lunar orbit to Earth’s orbit in a matter of minutes. A trip that would take… days??? Man, these people are bad at size.

They COULD have had inertial dampeners, and gravity plating, but Inception came out a little bit before this, so…

Kirk goes into a super radiation place to try and save Enterprise and stop it from falling. I wish there was Super Mutant like “I won’t go in there and rob you of your destiny”. He reactivates the warp core and they manage to survive a scene designed to be in the trailers/on the posters.

Remember this scene from Wrath of Khan?! It’s back! In backwards form. Oh boy oh boy. I’m surprised I feel nothing but damn, their friendship is poorly conveyed.

This movie feels like it was written by AI, and it was made in the early 2010s.

Khan’s ship falls to Earth and slams into Sanfran, toppling buildings and killing MILLIONS. This is the CAPITAL OF THE FEDERATION. Kirk has gotten so many god damn people killed.

We get a street chase through scenic Sanfran. How did Khan survive the crash? How did no one stop him leaving his ship? He’s Super Human, I guess.

Earlier in the movie, we saw Bones inject a dead tribble with Khan’s blood. Why did he do this? Do not ask questions. It’s brought the tribble back to life, but only Khan’s magic blood can do this. Too bad Spock is currently fist fighting Khan on a fucking ship flying over Sanfran. He’s losing. Then Uhura beams down and shoots Khan over and over. The Uhura maneuver!

Kirk is brought back. Okay so, this movie A) cures death B) allows you to teleport across glactic proportions. The implications of this are nuts.

Khan is frozen and put in The Indiana Jones room. Chrimminy. We then get a voice over with a speech by Kirk, who SHOULD be court martial’d for getting millions killed in Sanfran. Enterprise is recommissioned and fixed up and we’re all good. Oh hey, Marcus joined the crew! (she will not be back in the sequel). Kirk says Punch It, Chewie, and then the movie ends.

Wow this movie was bad. While 09 was at least kind of entertaining despite being incompetent, this isn’t even that. God, it gave me a fucking migraine, it’s SO FLASHY. Look, I know making fun of this movie and saying it’s bad, the worst Trek movie ever and a straight up terrible movie, is VERY common. Almost cliche. But it is, in fact, that bad. Do not watch this. Thankfully, every single thing in this movie can be ignored if you watch Beyond! Which is actually okay compared to this. So, y’know.
entry.summary: Every Sunday my Twitch subs and I watch Star Trek in my Discord. Here are my reviews and thoughts on each of the episodes as I see it. If you’re curious about a nerd’s views on Star Trek episodes, please read on, I go quite in depth with some of these episodes! I’ve watched some Star Trek before but not all of it!

entry.title: Retro Achievement Mastery 214 - Hello Kitty Magical Museum

link.title: Retro Achievement Mastery 214 - Hello Kitty Magical Museum

entry.content:
I have often said, publicly and privately, that the Gameboy Color is one of the worst systems in terms of averages. I can think of like, 4 must play games. Meanwhile, 40% of the library are things like this. Weird cashgrab media tie-ins.

That’s not to downplay the power of Hello Kitty. She’s a multimedia empire for a reason. But even though there have been countless Hello Kitty videogames, and some are actually kinda good, how many people are REALLY chomping at the bit for more? You MIGHT get something good. Or you could get something like this, which is clearly designed to sell more Hello Kitty merch.

This is a very simple game where you have a maze with balls moving around that’ll hurt you and make you reset. You need to pick up all the objects and then reach the exit. That’s it. Dodge the very easy and simple timing puzzles. At the end, you’re rewarded with an image of Hello Kitty.

My favorite one I saw was this. Hello Kitty with a tennis racket with a shirt that PROBABLY is supposed to say “I Love Tennis”, but is NOT centered correctly for her to say “I love Tennis” so it looks very suspiciously like something else.

This game isn’t BAD. But it’s not really much of anything. I’d say it’s best for very young children obsessed with Hello Kitty, and no one else. Or people who collect Sanrio merch.

Also hello, I stopped making these for a few months cause I kind of burned out on Retro Achievements, but I’m slowly getting back to it so it’s time to finish my backlog.
